title: Chief Officer, Office of Early Childhood Education
notes: Largest district in IL (~322K students). Office of Early Childhood Education at 42 W. Madison St, Chicago. CPS runs the largest PFA program in IL and Head Start grantee. New Superintendent/CEO Dr. Macquline King selected March 2026 (leadership transition). McKinily is Chief Officer of Early Childhood Ed since 2019; oversees Head Start and Preschool for All. Smart Start IL participant and major Universal PreK 2027 stakeholder. CUSD type.

title: Assistant Superintendent of Elementary and Early Childhood
notes: CUSD - ~26,000 students across Aurora/Naperville/Plainfield/Bolingbrook (DuPage + Will). Operates one early learning center (Addison ELC). Rosenblum is the senior owner of ECE strategy. District office 780 Shoreline Dr, Aurora 60504; main line 630-375-3000. Major IL district - high priority. PFA grantee.

title: Assistant Superintendent for Educational Services
notes: HSD - 9-12 only, no PreK. NOT an ECE prospect. Township HS serving Glenview/Northbrook/Northfield (~5,000 students). Superintendent: Dr. R.J. Gravel. Williamson oversees C&I but for high school only. Flag as low priority for ECE outreach.

title: Coordinator of Early Childhood, Special Education, MTSS & 504
notes: K-8 SD - ECE prospect. Director of C&I Dan Swartz (joined from Mundelein 75). Elizabeth Meyer School (K-1 ECE building) - Principal Helen Wei. PFA grantee. CIA team supports ECE. Newer admin leadership team posted 2024.

title: Director of Early Learning
notes: K-8 SD - strong ECE prospect. Known Teaching Strategies (Creative Curriculum) customer per case study published by TS. PFA grantee. Hispanic-majority district 15 miles west of Chicago. Thompson is dedicated, named ECE leader - rare for small district.

title: Assistant Principal & Early Childhood Coordinator
notes: K-8 SD in DuPage County. Preschool runs out of Forest Glen Elementary; Kindergarten Center is separate. Carolyn Jodelka is Preschool Parent Teacher Coordinator (cjodelka@d41.org). District office 793 N. Main Street, Glen Ellyn 60137.

title: Director of Curriculum & Instruction
notes: CCSD K-8 in McHenry County. Operates Oak Knoll Early Childhood Center. PFA half-day bilingual program. Principal Lisa Baker leads PFA at Oak Knoll. Superintendent Dr. Brandon C. White. Rivera owns ECE curriculum decisions.

title: Superintendent
notes: K-8 ESD in Lake County. Operates Lincoln Early Learning Center (multi-district PreK co-funded by D75 + Fremont SD 79 + Hawthorn CCSD 73) and Washington Early Learning Center. New Superintendent Dr. Corey Tafoya started July 1, 2025 (came from Harvard CUSD 50). Asst Supt Student Services: Jamie DiCarlo. Note: Skokie 73.5's Dan Swartz came from this district. Strong ECE infrastructure - good prospect.

title: Assistant Superintendent of Teaching and Learning
notes: CUSD - operates Barrington Early Learning Center (free PFA preschool at 40 E. Dundee Road, 224-770-4300). PFA grantee. Byrne owns PreK-12 curriculum. Asst Supt of Schools: Dr. Josh Carpenter. Strong wealthy suburban CUSD - high priority ECE prospect.

title: Early Childhood Coordinator & Assistant Principal, Greenbrier
notes: K-8 SD - strong ECE prospect. Community Preschool at Greenbrier Elementary; Preschool for All at Ivy Hill Elementary. ECDEC screening 847-963-3450. PFA grantee. Karen Pilli is contact at Greenbrier for 3-4yo programs.

title: Assistant Superintendent of Student Services, Equity & Inclusion
notes: K-8 SD - wealthy North Shore. Partners with three local preschools rather than operating its own PreK. Member of TrueNorth Cooperative (formerly NSSED) for screenings. Limited direct ECE programming - district outsources. Matthews oversees student services incl. early childhood screening (ages 2y10m-5y).

title: Director, District 109 Early Learners Program & Asst Principal South Park
notes: K-8 SD in Lake County. Early Learners Program located at South Park Elementary (1421 Hackberry Road, Deerfield 60015). Marcie Faust is South Park Principal (mfaust@dps109.org). Matt Zidron also listed as Program Director (mzidron@dps109.org x2105). PFA grantee.

title: Principal, Urbana Early Childhood School (prior); now MTSS Interventionist/Coach
notes: K-12 unit. Urbana Early Childhood School (UECS) at 2202 E. Washington St, Urbana 61802. Uses Creative Curriculum (Teaching Strategies). Leadership transition: Heinhorst moved to MTSS coaching role; Beth Ladd / Katie Madigan named as recent UECS principal. UECS earned Gold Circle of Quality rating from ExceleRate IL. PFA grantee. CUEarly partnership with C-U School District. Verify current UECS principal before outreach.

title: Superintendent
notes: K-8 SD (~464 students) in Tazewell County, south of Peoria. Operates B.E.A.R.S. Preschool. Marquette Elementary principal Jennifer Dietrich leads PreK-3rd. District office at Georgetowne Middle School, 51 Yates Road, Marquette Heights 61554. Small district - superintendent owns ECE decisions.

title: Curriculum & Technology Director
notes: CUSD (Congerville-Eureka-Goodfield), ~1,523 students PK-12 in Woodford County. Superintendent: Patrick Durley (durleyp@district140.org). Wherley owns curriculum incl. ECE. Small rural CUSD.

title: Superintendent
notes: Union K-12 district in Fulton County. Serves Early Childhood through HS. Sivertsen is 1998 Canton HS grad, district staff since 2008. District office: 20 West Walnut St, Canton 61520. Julie Tarvin on staff with ECE involvement. PFA grantee.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD ~1,400 students in DeKalb County. Wagner started July 1, 2024 from Elmwood CUSD 322 (25+ yrs experience). Recent leadership turnover (Brent O'Daniell resigned 2024; Matt Cascio interim before Wagner). Departments include Learning & Teaching. 95% HS grad rate.

title: Superintendent
notes: CCSD K-8 in LaSalle County. PFA grantee - 2 PFA classrooms + 1 ECSE Developmental Program (free). Cox previously superintendent of Erie IL. District at 1806 Guiles Ave, Mendota 61342. Good small-district ECE prospect.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD K-12 in Whiteside County. Leadership transition: Everett retires June 1, 2028; Asst Supt Dr. Sara Dail (sdail@sps5.org) named successor. New $11M indoor student recreation/training center groundbreaking Oct 2025. District 410 E Le Fevre Rd, Sterling 61081. Dail likely better curriculum contact.

title: Superintendent
notes: Unit K-12 in Jo Daviess County (NW IL). Vincent's background is curriculum (former DeKalb C&I director) - high relevance for ECE pitch. Small rural district. Note board president resigned shortly after his hire (per Telegraph Herald).

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD PreK-12 in Winnebago County (N IL). 97% grad rate. District at 200 W South St, Durand 61024. Small district - superintendent owns curriculum decisions. Note: petition history requesting Alberstett's resignation - verify current status.

title: Director of Curriculum, Instruction and Assessment
notes: K-12 SD in Morgan County (central IL). Operates Early Years Program (dedicated PreK building) - Mary English is a Director (mary.english@jsd117.org likely). Crossroads Learning Center is alternative. Raynor owns curriculum incl. PreK. PFA grantee.

title: Director of Special Education and School Support
notes: CUSD K-12, Madison County (Metro East). Major leadership transition: Dr. Allen Duncan Jr. interim superintendent (appointed Jan 12 2026); Dr. Patrick Shelton previously superintendent (left Sept 25, 2025). Preschool programs: Preschool Academy (tuition, full-day), Preschool for All (PFA grant funded), Early Childhood Special Education - all under Grotefendt's SpEd umbrella. District at 708 Saint Louis St, Edwardsville 62025. Verify Duncan/Shelton status before outreach.

title: Superintendent
notes: Unit K-12 in St Clair County (Metro East). Major equity district - majority-minority, Title I-heavy. Has Deputy Supt of Instruction, Asst Supt of C&I, Director of C&I (names not surfaced). Active labor dispute Feb 2026 - teachers union demanded investigation. District at 1700 Jerome Ln, Cahokia 62206. Verify McCall still in role given turmoil.

title: Superintendent (transition)
notes: CUSD K-12 in Madison County. Major leadership transition: superintendent vacancy posted, new sup to start July 1, 2026. Kreutztrager is incumbent. District has Director of C&I (name not surfaced). District at 401 Chaffer Ave, Roxana 62084. Timing note: best outreach window is post-July 2026 once new sup in place. Verify current sup before outreach.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD K-12 in St Clair County. Deets started July 1, 2023. District at 421 W Harnett, Mascoutah 62258. Recent middle school addition/renovation (Poettker Construction). Mid-size CUSD with curriculum dept under superintendent oversight.

title: Superintendent
notes: K-8 elementary SD in Marion County (S central IL). Hawthorn and Franklin Park schools. Foppe is single point of contact for curriculum decisions. ISPA member (Illinois Student Privacy Alliance). PFA-eligible. Small district.

title: Director of Early Childhood Education
notes: CUSD PreK-12 in Effingham County. STRONG ECE prospect: Unit 40 acquired Little Lambs Early Childhood Center - Fox led integration. Superintendent Andy Johnson retires June 30, 2028 (transition planning). District at 1000 W Grove Ave, Effingham 62401. 4 schools earned Commendable designation. Recent ECE expansion via Little Lambs acquisition is major buying signal.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD PK-12 (~1,519 students) in Champaign County. Includes Unity West Elementary. Home of the Rockets. District at 1121 County Rd 800 N, Tolono 61880. Small/mid rural CUSD - superintendent owns curriculum decisions.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD K-12 (~613 students) in Champaign County. Note official domain is fisherk12.org (not .com per input). 'Fisher Bunnies' mascot. District at 801 S 5th St, Fisher 61843. Small rural CUSD - superintendent is direct decision-maker.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD K-12 (~1,257 students) in Shelby County (central IL). 4 schools. District office at 720 W. Main Street; meetings at Shelbyville HS (1001 W. N. Sixth Street). $11,609/student spend. Small district - superintendent is decision-maker.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD K-12 in Sangamon County (south of Springfield). District at 606 W. North St, Auburn 62615. Small rural CUSD - superintendent is direct decision-maker.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD K-12 in Logan County. Spang started July 1, 2024 (succeeded Todd Hamm). Small rural CUSD - superintendent owns all curriculum decisions.

title: Principal/Superintendent
notes: Tiny CCSD K-8 (106 students, 1 school) in Franklin County (S IL). McCollum is dual-role principal/superintendent. District at 21962 Akin Blacktop, Thompsonville 62890. Note .com vs .org domain dual-listing. ECE limited but PFA possibly available.

title: Superintendent
notes: K-8 SD in Cook County (Alsip/Hazelgreen/Oak Lawn). STRONG ECE prospect: operates dedicated District 126 Early Childhood Center (PreK only, 117 students, full-day and half-day, ages 3-5, special ed inclusion) at 12250 S Orchard Ave, Alsip 60803 (708-631-0490). Leadership transition: Gwaltney retires June 2027; Dr. Martelli named successor. PFA grantee. High-priority ECE prospect.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD K-12 (327 students, 2 schools) in Henry County. Bryan hired June 2022 from Kewanee HS (15 yr principal). District at 501 W South St, Annawan 61234. 'Braves' mascot may face IL mascot ban legislation. Small rural CUSD - superintendent is decision-maker.

title: Interim Superintendent / Principal
notes: CHSD - 9-12 ONLY, NO PreK. NOT an ECE prospect. Township HS in Summit IL serving 7329 W 63rd St. Major leadership crisis: prior Supt William Toulios resigned Nov 2025 after credit card misuse investigation. Cotter is acting interim. Flag as low priority for ECE outreach.

title: Interim Superintendent / HS Principal
notes: CUSD PK-12 (272 students, 3 schools) in Fulton County. Major crisis: prior Supt Dr. Tori Lindeman released Feb 2026 after sexual misconduct/harassment investigation. Phillips is Astoria graduate, former Brown County CUSD #1 superintendent. District at 402 N Jefferson St, Astoria 61501. Sensitive timing - district in transition.

title: Superintendent
notes: Single-school K-8 SD in Lake County (small affluent village of Bannockburn). District at 2165 Telegraph Road, Bannockburn 60015. Very small district - superintendent is direct decision-maker. Limited dedicated ECE staffing typical for size.

title: Superintendent
notes: CUSD PreK-12 (~1,063 students, 3 schools) in Will County (S Chicagoland). District at 538 Miller St, Beecher 60401. Some conflicting source data lists Tami Roskamp or Vicki Ploense as superintendent - verify before outreach. Small mid-size CUSD.
